title: centaurea ambigua subsp. nigra
intro: Centaurea ambigua subsp. nigra, also known as the Black Knapweed, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Centaurea ambigua subsp. nigra include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress. Herbal preparations of Centaurea ambigua subsp. nigra include infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
